title: Nothocissus behrmannii
taxonomy: Nothocissus behrmannii is a species of flowering plant in the family Ruscaceae. It is native to New Guinea and is a type of evergreen climber. Nothocissus behrmannii is closely related to the genus Colchicum and is often placed within the same family, Liliaceae. However, recent studies have reclassified it within the family Ruscaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as butterbur or lilyturf. The exact classification of Nothocissus behrmannii is still a subject of ongoing research in the field of botany.

intro: Nothocissus behrmannii, also known as the "Tasmanian sarsaparilla," is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the Smilacaceae family. The roots of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, as well as being used to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin issues.
